title: Identity Matrix
description:
Implement `eye(j)`, which returns the $j \times j$ identity matrix:

$$\text{eye}(j)_{r,c} = \begin{cases} 1 & r = c \\ 0 & r \neq c \end{cases}$$

**Input**: `j` (python int, the size). **Output**: shape `[j, j]`.

Solve with broadcasting only — compare two `torch.arange(j)` vectors against each other, no `torch.eye`.
starter_code:
import torch

def eye(j):
    # Return the j x j identity matrix without calling torch.eye.
    pass
hints: Solve in 1 line using broadcasting only.
Compare a column vector torch.arange(j)[:, None] against a row vector torch.arange(j)[None, :] for equality.

solutions.name: Broadcasted row/column equality
solutions.explanation: Broadcasting `arange(j)` as a column against `arange(j)` as a row produces a $[j,j]$ grid; the entry at $(r,c)$ is `True` exactly when $r = c$, so casting the equality mask to an integer dtype gives the identity matrix.
